title: nodemailer createTransport with tls.rejectUnauthorized: false — the SMTP server certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The transport validates the SMTP server certificate (tls.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the mail connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: createTransport is configured with tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the SMTP server's TLS certificate. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the mail connection — capturing SMTP credentials and the contents of every email sent (password resets, invites, magic links). Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust a private CA via tls.ca if needed.
params.absentCallDetail: No createTransport call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: createTransport does not set tls.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted to get past a self-signed cert on an internal SMTP relay and left in. It disables MITM protection on the channel that carries password-reset and magic-link emails — the keys to every account.
